One of our residents did a thesis that you can actually predict post-operative hydrocephalus in children of spina bifida by doing a pre-operative cranial ultrasound.

And for the interest of the radiology consultants, if the bifrontal diameter of the lateral ventricles is more than 26 millimeters, the bicordate diameter more than 20 millimeters, and the body of lateral ventricle diameter more than 26 millimeters, then the chances of developing postoperative hydrocephalus are statistically more than 98%.
This has been verified in a series of 400 patients by logistic regression and has been published in Child's Nervous System.
